contents:
# US deploys additional F-35 squadron to Romania amid Russia border tensions

## Overview
The US Air Force rotated 12 F-35A Lightning II jets to eastern flank NATO base in response to Russian troop buildup near Ukraine. Deployment enhances air policing over Black Sea amid CEPA-reported Chinese drone supplies to Moscow.

## Details
Squadron from 48th Fighter Wing, RAF Lakenheath, UK, arrived via 4 C-17 transports. Involves 200 personnel and AIM-120D missiles. NATO's Enhanced Air Policing mission extended through June. "Strengthens deterrence against aggression," said US EUCOM commander Gen. Christopher Cavoli. Russian Su-35s intercepted nearby same day. CEPA tweet linked it to transatlantic burden-sharing push. No incidents reported.

**Location**: Mihail Kogalniceanu Air Base, Romania

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @cepa, which covers CEPA European security Russia China NATO transatlantic. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following CEPA European security Russia China NATO transatlantic will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
